rationale: Both evaluations return an identical 2/10 score and reach the same substantive conclusions: the candidate lacks the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, lacks enterprise SQL Server/Oracle expertise, and lacks domain depth in Fixed Income/Capital Markets/Investment Banking, while crediting 15+ years of experience, TDD/CI/CD strength, and brief financial exposure via RIDE Capital. The wording differs (e.g., 'wealth tech' vs 'investment practices', 'Derivatives' vs 'Capital Markets'), but these are ordinary run-to-run rephrasings that key off the same resume facts, not the name change. No reasoning attaches to the candidate being named James Smith rather than Bogdan Szabo.
